💰 Step-by-Step Guide to UK Farm Grants 2026

Understanding the Post-Brexit Grant Landscape

The old Basic Payment Scheme (BPS) is ending. New Environmental Land Management (ELM) schemes are here. This guide explains what's available and how to apply.

Current Grant Schemes

### 1. Sustainable Farming Incentive (SFI) 2026 What it is: Payments for environmentally friendly farming practices Payment rates (examples): | Action | Payment per hectare | |--------|---------------------| | Improved grassland | £151/ha | | Moorland | £63/ha | | Hedgerow management | £5/100m | | Pollinator mix | £615/ha | Eligibility: - Minimum 3 hectares agricultural land - Must be registered with RPA - One SFI agreement per business - Maximum £100,000/year Application windows: - June 2026: Small farms/those without existing ELM - September 2026: All farms How to apply: 1. Log into Rural Payments service 2. Check your land is registered 3. Choose actions that suit your farm 4. Submit application 5. Receive agreement within 3 months ### 2. Farming Investment Fund (FIF) What it is: Grants for equipment and technology Themes: - FETF (Equipment & Tech) - £1,000-£25,000 - Slurry Infrastructure - Up to £250,000 - Water Management - Up to £500,000 - Adding Value - Up to £500,000 2026 Deadlines: | Round | Opens | Closes | |-------|-------|--------| | FETF 2026 | 17 March | 27 April | | Water Management | TBC | TBC | Application process: 1. Expression of interest (online) 2. Full application (if shortlisted) 3. Approval and claim process 4. Installation and verification ### 3. Countryside Stewardship (CS) Higher Tier: - For complex environmental land management - 5-20 year agreements - Requires pre-application advice - 142 capital items available Mid Tier: - Simpler options - 5-year agreements - Apply online Capital Grants: - Standalone grants (don't need full CS) - Fencing, tree planting, water provision - New round opens July 2026 ### 4. Brexit Support - Delinked Payments Replacing BPS: - No application needed - Automatic payments based on historical claims - Phasing out: 98% reduction on first £30k by 2027

Step-by-Step Application Guide

### Before You Apply 1. Check eligibility: - [ ] Registered with RPA - [ ] Land registered on Rural Land Register - [ ] No outstanding cross compliance issues - [ ] Meet scheme-specific requirements 2. Gather documents: - [ ] SBI (Single Business Identifier) - [ ] Field numbers and maps - [ ] Business bank statements - [ ] Previous grant agreements (if applicable) 3. Plan your application: - Which schemes suit your farm? - What actions can you realistically deliver? - Calculate income vs. effort required ### During Application 1. Use correct portals: - SFI: Rural Payments service - FIF: DEFRA online portal - CS: Natural England application 2. Be accurate: - Double-check field numbers - Verify land areas - Include all required evidence 3. Save regularly: - Applications can take hours - Save progress every 15 minutes - Screenshot confirmation page ### After Submission 1. Record keeping: - Save application reference number - Take screenshots of submission - Keep copies of all documents 2. Response timeline: - SFI: 3 months - FIF: 3-6 months - CS Higher: 6+ months 3. If successful: - Read agreement carefully - Note reporting requirements - Set calendar reminders for claims

Common Mistakes to Avoid

❌ Applying for unsuitable actions - Only choose what you can realistically deliver - Consider your farm type and resources ❌ Incorrect field numbers - Always verify with RLR maps - Mismatches cause delays/rejection ❌ Missing deadlines - Set multiple reminders - Submit 1 week before deadline ❌ Poor record keeping - SFI requires 5 years of records - Photograph everything - Keep all receipts ❌ Not understanding agreements - Read terms and conditions - Know your obligations - Understand penalty clauses

Quick Reference: 2026 Deadlines

| Scheme | Deadline | Action | |--------|----------|--------| | FETF 2026 | 27 April 2026 | Apply now | | Farming Resilience | 15 March 2026 | Urgent | | SFI 2026 Window 1 | June 2026 | Plan now | | SFI 2026 Window 2 | September 2026 | Plan later | | CS Capital Grants | July 2026 | Watch for opening |

Useful Resources

- Rural Payments: ruralpayments.defra.gov.uk - Defra Blog: defra.gov.uk - NFU Guidance: nfuonline.com - FWAG Advice: fwag.org.uk

Get Help

- RPA Helpline: 03000 200 301 - NFU CallFirst: 0370 845 8458 - Local AHDB: ahdb.org.uk - Agricultural consultant: Find at iagrm.org.uk *Grants can transform your farm business – but only if you apply correctly and deliver what you promise.* *Last updated: 26 February 2026*
